creator: jim@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Sun, 09 Sep 2001 at 21:59:06 +0200, Jochem Kossen wrote:
 > 
 > >Submitter-Id:	current-users
 > >Originator:	Jochem Kossen
 > >Organization:	
 > >Confidential:	no
 > >Synopsis:	pkg_plist of net/gaim incorrectly specified
 > >Severity:	serious
 > >Priority:	medium
 > >Category:	ports
 > >Class:		sw-bug
 > >Release:	FreeBSD 4.4-RC i386
 > >Environment:
 > System: FreeBSD lisa 4.4-RC FreeBSD 4.4-RC #0: Fri Sep 7 23:49:53 CEST 2001 jochem@lisa:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/LISA i386
 > >Description:
 > 	either pkg_plist of net/gaim (version 0.43) is incorrectly specified, or the files are installed
 > 	in an incorrect directory. When i do a 'make deinstall', for all the pixmaps, i get these kind of errors:
 > 
 > 	pkg_delete: file `/usr/X11R6/share/gnome/pixmaps/gaim/cancel.xpm' doesn't really exist
 > 
 > 	The pixmaps are actually installed in /usr/X11R6/share/pixmaps/gaim/
 > >How-To-Repeat:
 > 	cd /usr/ports/net/gaim && make install && make deinstall
 > >Fix:
 
 Hmm.  I can't replicate this.  Do you have any GNOME bits installed at
 all (if your answer to this is yes, then it will build with GNOME
 support).  I'm guessing it's something GNOME-related, but not having a
 box with GNOME on it doesn't leave me with much more than guesses...
 
 - jim
